purpose: Define Cinepolis-specific reimbursement mapping from HR Boleto and Combo columns, payment batch item selection, individual movement ledger entries and historic payment reconciliation without executing real payments.

sourceReferences.notes: These are source names described by Paula. This contract does not ingest or store raw files.

cinepolisReimbursementFields.totalRule: reimbursementTotal = boletoAmount + comboAmount unless project/country rule overrides with audit note

batchRules: Admin must be able to select which eligible payment items are included in a batch.
A batch can include selected shoppers/visits only, not automatically every eligible item.
Each selected item must keep shopperId, visitId/hrRowId, honorarium, boleto, combo, reimbursementTotal, currency and status.
A batch can be prepared, reviewed, approved, paid, closed or reopened according to policy.
Batch payment does not collapse individual movements. Individual movement entries must remain visible and traceable.
Historical payments from May and July sources must reconcile against items to avoid duplicate payment.
Payment item can belong to one active paid batch only. Reversals/adjustments require explicit movement entries.
movementRules: Every paid item must create or map to an individual financial movement.
Movement must show batchId and paymentItemId.
Movement must show what it paid: honorarium, boleto, combo and total.
Movement must show source: batch payment, historical import, manual adjustment or reversal.
Movement must preserve paidAt/manualPaidAt, paidBy/manualPaidBy and auditTrail when runtime is authorized.
Shopper-facing movement view must not expose bank details or internal audit notes.
historicalReconciliationRules: Historical payment records from May and July must be imported/mapped as paid movements or source payment refs before new batch closure.
Do not duplicate paid items already present in historical sources.
If historical source lacks stable visitId/hrRowId, route to manual review rather than visual matching only.
Use tenantId, projectId, shopperId, periodId, hrRowId/visitId, amount and sourceRef for reconciliation candidates.

newLessons: How Boleto and Combo reimbursements work
How to select items into a payment batch
Why individual movements remain visible after batch payment
How historical May/July payments are reconciled
How shoppers see benefits without bank data

hardStops: Do not execute real payments from this contract.
Do not write Firestore from this contract.
Do not write HR from this contract.
Do not import raw source files from this contract.
Do not duplicate historical May/July paid items.
Do not expose bank data in shopper-visible movements.
